anchors.title: Story development beats essay shape
anchors.decision_rule: Prefer stronger development of events, character, setting, conflict, voice, or reflection over a tidy but flat sequence.

anchors.title: Narrative purpose matters
anchors.decision_rule: A rougher story with clearer meaning, tension, or character change can outrank a cleaner story with little effect.

anchors.title: Conventions matter when readability breaks
anchors.decision_rule: Surface errors are decisive only when they interfere with following the story or when development is otherwise close.

caution_checks: Which narrative creates a clearer experience for the reader?
Is the cleaner story underdeveloped or merely a sequence of events?
Does the rougher story remain understandable?
